questions.q: In Hindu tradition, Nandi is the vahana (mount) of which deity?
questions.explanation: Nandi is the sacred bull who serves as the vahana of Shiva.

questions.q: What kind of animal is Nandi, the mount of Shiva?
questions.explanation: Nandi is depicted as a bull, the vahana of Shiva.

questions.q: Nandi, the bull of Shiva, is traditionally described as being what colour?
questions.explanation: Nandi is traditionally described as a white bull.

questions.q: In a Shaiva temple, where is the seated sculpture of Nandi usually placed?
options: Facing the sanctum that houses Shiva
At the rear of the temple
questions.explanation: A seated Nandi sculpture faces the sanctum, eternally gazing upon the Lord.

questions.q: Nandi is regarded as the foremost devotee of which god?
questions.explanation: Nandi is honoured as the foremost devotee of Shiva.

questions.q: Where do Shaiva devotees traditionally whisper their prayers so that they may reach Shiva?
questions.explanation: Devotees traditionally whisper their prayers into Nandi's ear to carry them to Shiva.

questions.q: Besides being Shiva's mount, what role does Nandi serve at the entrance to Shiva's abode?
questions.explanation: Nandi is the gatekeeper of Shiva as well as his mount and devotee.

questions.q: Which great ascetic god, dwelling on Mount Kailash, rides the bull Nandi?
questions.explanation: Shiva, the great ascetic of Mount Kailash, has Nandi as his vahana.

questions.q: Nandi is described as the chief of which group of attendants upon Shiva?
questions.explanation: Nandi is described as the chief of Shiva's ganas, his attendant hosts.

questions.q: When a worshipper enters a Shiva temple, in which direction is the Nandi statue typically positioned relative to the Shiva lingam?
questions.explanation: Nandi is seated facing the sanctum, gazing toward Shiva enshrined as the lingam.

questions.q: The name 'Nandi' is most closely associated with which quality in Sanskrit tradition?
questions.explanation: The name Nandi is connected with the sense of joy or delight (from the root nand).

questions.q: Seated before the sanctum of a Shiva temple, what does the Nandi statue eternally gaze upon?
questions.explanation: Nandi, seated before the sanctum, eternally gazes upon Shiva worshipped as the lingam.

questions.q: Nandi serves as the bull mount of Shiva. Which deity's mount is the eagle Garuda rather than Nandi?
questions.explanation: Nandi is the mount of Shiva alone; Vishnu's vahana is the eagle Garuda, not Nandi.

questions.q: The bull is also the traditional symbol of which concept that Nandi is said to embody?
questions.explanation: The bull, like Nandi, is the enduring symbol of dharma, the righteousness that upholds the cosmos.

questions.q: Parvati is the consort of the lord whom Nandi serves as mount. Who is that lord?
questions.explanation: Parvati is the consort of Shiva, the lord whom Nandi serves as mount and devotee.

questions.q: What is the signature weapon of Shiva, the god whose mount is Nandi?
questions.explanation: The Trishula (trident) is the signature weapon of Shiva, whose mount is Nandi.

questions.q: On which Himalayan mountain, the abode of Shiva, would Nandi be found attending his lord?
questions.explanation: Nandi attends Shiva at Mount Kailash, the lord's eternal Himalayan abode.

questions.q: In iconography, Nandi is most often depicted in what posture before the shrine of Shiva?
options: Seated and couchant, facing the shrine
questions.explanation: Nandi is most often shown seated and couchant, calmly facing the sanctum of Shiva.

questions.q: What is the relationship of Nandi to Shiva, according to Shaiva tradition?
questions.explanation: Nandi is at once Shiva's mount (vahana), foremost devotee, and gatekeeper.

questions.q: The serpent coiled at the throat of Shiva, the god Nandi serves, is named what?
questions.explanation: Vasuki, king of serpents, coils at Shiva's throat, the lord whose mount is Nandi.

questions.q: Which elephant-headed son of Shiva and Parvati, who rides a mouse, belongs to the household that Nandi serves?
questions.explanation: Ganesha, the elephant-headed son of Shiva and Parvati, rides a mouse and belongs to Shiva's household that Nandi serves.

questions.q: In Shaiva devotional practice, Nandi is venerated chiefly as an exemplar of which virtue?
questions.explanation: Nandi is venerated as the exemplar of steadfast, unwavering devotion to Shiva.

questions.q: In Shaiva tradition, Nandi is sometimes regarded as a teacher who transmitted Shiva's wisdom; he is therefore counted as the foremost of which group?
questions.explanation: Nandi is reckoned the chief of Shiva's ganas and a transmitter of his teachings.

questions.q: The colossal Nandi at the great Brihadisvara temple in Thanjavur is famed for being carved from what?
options: A single massive block of stone
questions.explanation: The colossal Nandi at the Brihadisvara temple in Thanjavur is renowned as a monolith carved from a single block of stone.

questions.q: Among the hosts that attend Shiva, what rank does Nandi hold?
options: The leader and chief of the ganas
questions.explanation: Nandi is the leader and chief among Shiva's ganas, his closest attendant host.

questions.q: Nandi is closely linked in iconography to the bull that serves as the symbol of which principle personified in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The bull is the enduring symbol of Dharma personified, an association reflected in Nandi.

questions.q: In temple ritual, why is it traditionally said that a devotee should view the Shiva lingam past the figure of Nandi?
options: Because Nandi guards a hidden treasure
Because Nandi forever faces and beholds his Lord
questions.explanation: Nandi is positioned eternally facing Shiva, so the devotee shares the bull's perpetual gaze upon the Lord.

questions.q: Which name, meaning 'one whose mount is the bull', is applied to Shiva on account of his association with Nandi?
questions.explanation: Shiva is called Vrishabhavahana, 'he whose mount is the bull', from his vahana Nandi.

questions.q: Nandi's role as the one who carries devotees' messages to Shiva most directly reflects his function as Shiva's what?
questions.explanation: As gatekeeper and intermediary, Nandi conveys the whispered prayers of devotees to Shiva.

questions.q: Across South Indian Shaiva temples, the presence of a dedicated Nandi mandapa (pavilion) before the sanctum chiefly signifies what?
questions.explanation: A Nandi mandapa facing the sanctum is a hallmark of a Shiva temple, since Nandi is Shiva's mount and gatekeeper.
